platform: custom tube (no Cloudflare; accessible via stdlib urllib)

metadata_mapping.date: JSON-LD VideoObject > uploadDate (ISO-8601 datetime; strip to YYYY-MM-DD)
metadata_mapping.description: JSON-LD VideoObject > description  (may be 'No description provided'; treat as empty)
metadata_mapping.cover_image: JSON-LD VideoObject > thumbnailUrl  (plain string CDN URL, NOT an array)
metadata_mapping.performers: HTML: <a class="badge badge-video-info badge-kiss" href="/profile/{slug}"> anchor text  (badge-kiss = verified performer; exclude badge-unverified = uploader account)
metadata_mapping.tags: Category slug from scene URL first path segment, title-cased (e.g. amateur-porn -> Amateur Porn)

date: Value format: '2013-12-09T14:30:03+00:00' -- split on 'T' and take date part
description: If value equals 'No description provided' (case-insensitive), return empty string
cover_image: Value is a PLAIN STRING, not a list -- do not subscript with [0]
performers: Regex or BeautifulSoup: find <a> tags where class contains 'badge-kiss' and href starts with '/profile/'
Exclude any link whose class contains 'badge-unverified' (uploader account, not a performer)
tags: Parse category slug from scene URL: the first path segment after the domain
Convert: replace hyphens with spaces, apply str.title() -> 'Amateur Porn'

search_strategy.notes: GET request with query param 'what'. Returns HTML page with video card links. No JS challenge or blocking observed. Use SequenceMatcher to find best title match from results.

fragment_matching_strategy.notes: sceneByQueryFragment and sceneByFragment omitted -- fragment matching is unreliable without further testing.

anti_bot_or_access_notes: No Cloudflare or aggressive anti-bot protection observed.
Standard Firefox/Linux UA is sufficient for all page types.
Python urllib works directly; curl subprocess fallback kept per repo convention.
Scene pages, profile pages, and search all return 200 with a realistic UA.
known_limitations: No per-scene tag list -- only the category slug from the URL is available as a tag.
description is often empty ('No description provided') for user-uploaded content.
JSON-LD thumbnailUrl is a plain string (not an array); do not subscript it.
Performer profile pages have no structured bio fields (no birthdate, gender, country, measurements) -- only name and photo URL available.
